conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the official guidance to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your question! I'll help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit. To assess this properly, I need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have any children that you'd like to claim Child Benefit for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to claim Child Benefit. I have one child - my son Alex. He's 8 years old and lives with me. I'm just wanting to check if I'm eligible to claim for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information! It's helpful to know about Alex.

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask a few more questions:

Do you currently live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you. 

Just to confirm - you mentioned that Alex lives with you. Is Alex living with you full-time, or does he spend time living with anyone else (for example, another parent or relative)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He lives with me full-time, yes.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for confirming that.

One more question: Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for answering those questions.

Based on the information you've provided, let me assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex:

- You have a child (Alex) who is 8 years old (under 16)
- You live in the UK (Manchester)
- Alex lives with you full-time
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

This means you meet the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex. You are responsible for him, he lives with you, he's under 16, and only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child - which would be you in this case.

Let me provide you with the formal assessment now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news!** You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

You meet all the necessary requirements:
- Alex is 8 years old (under 16)
- You're responsible for him and he lives with you full-time
- You live in the UK
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him

You can go ahead and apply for Child Benefit. Please note that if either you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but you can still claim the benefit.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 11 weeks (within 12-week limit)
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child in hospital for 11 weeks (within 12-week limit); Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child in hospital for 11 weeks (within 12-week limit)
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit. The claimant meets all the required criteria: (1) Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, (2) The claimant is responsible for Alex as he lives with them full-time, (3) The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester), and (4) Nobody else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. According to the eligibility rules, a person normally qualifies for Child Benefit if they're responsible for a child under 16 and live in the UK, and they'll usually be responsible for a child if they live with them. Only one person can get Child Benefit for a child, and in this case, no one else is claiming.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for their 8-year-old son Alex. They meet all the standard eligibility requirements: they live in the UK, Alex is under 16, Alex lives with them full-time, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

Only one person can get Child Benefit for a child, and in this case, no one else is claiming.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for their 8-year-old son Alex. They meet all the standard eligibility requirements: they live in the UK, Alex is under 16, Alex lives with them full-time, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.
